reasoning: Bitcoin is currently trading near $111,600 as of late October 2025. The threshold for the question is $93,429.20 by December 31, 2025. This gives Bitcoin an ~18% buffer over the required closing price. With roughly 66 days left until the end of the year, Black-Scholes risk-neutral bounds with 50-60% annualized volatility place the probability of staying above the threshold at ~75%. When adjusting for the typical positive real-world drift of crypto assets, the probability rises to the low 80s, which is well-aligned with the Metaculus community's recent estimate of 0.85 on Oct 16. Bitcoin recently surged above $120k and cooled off, but it still has substantial breathing room, so a probability around 83% captures both the strong likelihood of succeeding and the characteristic downside volatility risk of crypto markets over a two-month horizon.
